name: gemma-code
description: Local agentic coding assistant powered by Gemma 4 via Ollama

gemma-code.maxTokens.description: Maximum context tokens (Gemma 4 supports 128K for E2B/E4B, 256K for 26B/31B)

gemma-code.toolConfirmationMode.description: When to prompt for confirmation before running terminal commands or editing files. 'always': always ask. 'ask': ask for destructive operations only. 'never': run without confirmation (use with caution).

gemma-code.maxAgentIterations.description: Maximum number of agentic tool-use iterations per user message before the loop terminates.

enumDescriptions: Apply file edits immediately without prompting.
Show a diff and ask for confirmation before applying any file edit.
Show diffs in the chat panel but never write files (review-only mode).
gemma-code.editMode.description: Controls how file edits (edit_file, write_file, create_file) are applied. 'auto': apply immediately. 'ask': show diff and confirm. 'manual': show diff only, never write.

gemma-code.thinkingMode.description: Enable Gemma 4 thinking mode (<|think|>) for chain-of-thought reasoning before tool use and complex responses.

enumDescriptions: Brief, direct instructions optimized for experienced developers.
Step-by-step instructions with technical term definitions for new developers.
gemma-code.promptStyle.description: Controls the verbosity and style of the system prompt sent to the model.

gemma-code.systemPromptBudgetPercent.description: Percentage of the context window reserved for the system prompt (base instructions + tool declarations). Range: 5-30%.

gemma-code.useBackend.description: When true, Gemma Code starts a local Python FastAPI backend that handles prompt formatting and context management. When false, the extension talks directly to Ollama.

gemma-code.backendPort.description: Port for the local Python inference backend. Change this if 11435 conflicts with another service.

gemma-code.pythonPath.description: Path to the Python executable used to start the backend (e.g. 'python3' or an absolute path to a virtual-environment interpreter).
